Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of Royaltech Limited, storing their contact and demographic information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the customer.

lastName.description: The last name of the customer.

email.description: The primary email address of the customer.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the customer.

address.description: The physical address of the customer.

companyName.description: The name of the company the customer is associated with, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

LaptopProduct.title: LaptopProduct
LaptopProduct.description: Represents a generic laptop product model, e.g., 'Dell Latitude 7420', defining its specifications and general details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LaptopProduct entity.

make.description: The manufacturer of the laptop (e.g., Dell, HP, Apple).

model.description: The specific model name or number of the laptop (e.g., Latitude 7420, MacBook Pro M2).

description.description: A detailed description of the laptop product, highlighting its features.

specifications.description: A JSON string or formatted text detailing the laptop's technical specifications (e.g., CPU, RAM, storage, screen size).

productImageUrl.description: URL to an image of the laptop product.

category.description: The category of the laptop (e.g., Business, Gaming, Ultrabook, Student).

LaptopInstance.title: LaptopInstance
LaptopInstance.description: Represents a specific, individual physical laptop unit with its unique serial number and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LaptopInstance entity.

laptopProductId.description: Reference to the LaptopProduct this instance belongs to. (Relationship: LaptopProduct 1:N LaptopInstance)

serialNumber.description: The unique serial number of this specific laptop unit.

purchaseDate.description: The date Royaltech Limited purchased this laptop instance.

warrantyEndDate.description: The end date of the manufacturer's warranty for this laptop instance.

status.description: The current status of the laptop instance (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Leased', 'Repair', 'Sold', 'Disposed').

LeaseAgreement.title: LeaseAgreement
LeaseAgreement.description: Tracks the details of a laptop lease agreement between Royaltech Limited and a customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeaseAgreement entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who is leasing the laptop. (Relationship: Customer 1:N LeaseAgreement)

laptopInstanceId.description: Reference to the specific LaptopInstance being leased. (Relationship: LaptopInstance 1:N LeaseAgreement)

startDate.description: The date the lease agreement begins.

endDate.description: The scheduled date the lease agreement ends.

monthlyPaymentAmount.description: The agreed-upon monthly payment amount for the lease.

totalLeaseAmount.description: The total financial amount for the entire lease term.

status.description: The current status of the lease agreement (e.g., 'Active', 'Completed', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled').

returnDateActual.description: The actual date the leased laptop was returned, if applicable.

leaseTermMonths.description: The duration of the lease agreement in months.

LeasePayment.title: LeasePayment
LeasePayment.description: Records individual payments made by a customer towards a lease agreement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeasePayment entity.

leaseAgreementId.description: Reference to the LeaseAgreement this payment is for. (Relationship: LeaseAgreement 1:N LeasePayment)

paymentDate.description: The date the payment was made.

amountPaid.description: The amount paid in this transaction.

expectedPaymentDate.description: The date this payment was originally expected.

Repair.title: Repair
Repair.description: Records details of a laptop repair, including the problem, resolution, and costs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Repair entity.

laptopInstanceId.description: Reference to the specific LaptopInstance undergoing repair. (Relationship: LaptopInstance 1:N Repair)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who initiated the repair request. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Repair)

reportedProblem.description: A description of the problem reported by the customer.

repairStartDate.description: The date when the repair work began.

repairEndDate.description: The date when the repair work was completed or resolved.

status.description: The current status of the repair (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

technicianNotes.description: Detailed notes from the technician regarding the diagnosis and repair steps taken.

cost.description: The total cost associated with the repair.

LocationTracking.title: LocationTracking
LocationTracking.description: Records the geographical location of a specific laptop instance at a given time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LocationTracking entry.

laptopInstanceId.description: Reference to the LaptopInstance whose location is being tracked. (Relationship: LaptopInstance 1:N LocationTracking)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the location data was recorded.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the laptop's location.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the laptop's location.

accuracy.description: The accuracy of the location data in meters, if available.

SalesTransaction.title: SalesTransaction
SalesTransaction.description: Records details of a direct sale of one or more laptops or services to a customer, used for generating receipts and invoices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SalesTransaction entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer involved in this sales transaction. (Relationship: Customer 1:N SalesTransaction)

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the sales transaction occurred.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the sales transaction.

status.description: The status of the sales transaction (e.g., 'Completed', 'Pending Payment', 'Cancelled', 'Refunded').

invoiceNumber.description: The unique invoice number generated for this transaction.

soldLaptopInstanceIds.description: An array of references to LaptopInstance entities sold in this transaction. (Relationship: LaptopInstance N:N SalesTransaction)

definition.description: Collection for Royaltech Limited's administrator roles. Existence of a document at this path grants administrative privileges. This uses 'Existence over Content' for role-based access control.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

definition.description: Collection for Royaltech Limited's staff roles. Existence of a document at this path grants staff-level privileges. This uses 'Existence over Content' for role-based access control.

params.name: staffUid
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the staff member.

definition.description: Stores records of customers. The document ID (`customerId`) is expected to be the Firebase Authentication UID of the customer for self-management. Includes the `createdAt` timestamp. Accessible by Royaltech staff/admins and by the customer themselves.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer, expected to match their Firebase Authentication UID for customer self-access.

definition.description: Stores generic laptop product models (e.g., 'Dell Latitude 7420'). This collection is primarily for internal management by Royaltech staff/admins.

params.name: laptopProductId
params.description: The unique identifier for a generic laptop product model.

definition.description: Stores specific, individual physical laptop units with unique serial numbers and current status (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Leased', 'Sold'). This collection is primarily for internal stock management by Royaltech staff/admins.

params.name: laptopInstanceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific physical laptop unit.

definition.description: Records details of lease agreements between Royaltech and customers. Includes `customerId` for authorization independence, allowing customers to view their own agreements directly. Accessible by Royaltech staff/admins and by the customer associated with the agreement.

params.name: leaseAgreementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a lease agreement.

definition.description: Records individual payments made by a customer towards a specific lease agreement. This document includes denormalized 'customerId' from its parent LeaseAgreement for Authorization Independence, enabling direct customer access rules without requiring a 'get()' call to the parent. Accessible by Royaltech staff/admins and by the customer associated with the payment.

params.name: leaseAgreementId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lease agreement.

params.name: leasePaymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an individual lease payment.

definition.description: Records details of laptop repairs, including reported problem, resolution, and costs. Includes `customerId` for authorization independence, allowing customers to view their own repair requests. Accessible by Royaltech staff/admins and by the customer who initiated the repair.

params.name: repairId
params.description: The unique identifier for a repair record.

definition.description: Records the geographical location of a specific laptop instance at a given time. This is primarily for internal asset tracking by Royaltech staff/admins due to privacy considerations.

params.name: locationTrackingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a location tracking entry.

definition.description: Records details of direct sales of laptops or services to a customer. Includes `customerId` for authorization independence, allowing customers to view their own transactions. Accessible by Royaltech staff/admins and by the customer involved in the transaction.

params.name: salesTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a sales transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Royaltech Limited is designed around stringent security, scalability, and debuggability principles. The primary goal is to ensure Authorization Independence by minimizing `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules, which enhances atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging. This is achieved through strategic denormalization and structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**
*   **Customer-facing Subcollections:** `LeasePayment` documents, residing in the subcollection `/lease_agreements/{leaseAgreementId}/payments/{leasePaymentId}`, will explicitly include a `customerId` field. This `customerId` will be denormalized (copied from its parent `LeaseAgreement` during creation). This allows security rules to evaluate customer ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerId`) directly on the `LeasePayment` document, eliminating the need to `get()` the parent `LeaseAgreement`. This is crucial for maintaining atomic operations and efficient rule processing.
*   **Top-level Customer-owned Collections:** `LeaseAgreement`, `Repair`, and `SalesTransaction` entities already include `customerId` as a top-level field as per their schemas. This design directly supports authorization independence, allowing customers to query and access *their own* data by filtering on `customerId` where `customerId == request.auth.uid`.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
The structure enables secure and efficient `list` operations (QAPs) by ensuring that necessary authorization fields are directly present in the queried documents:
*   **Employee Access:** Royaltech staff and administrators, identified by their presence in the `/roles_staff/{uid}` or `/roles_admin/{uid}` collections, have broad read/write access to all core business data collections (`customers`, `laptop_products`, `laptop_instances`, `lease_agreements`, `repairs`, `location_tracking`, `sales_transactions`). They can `list` all documents in these collections, as their role grants them full access.
*   **Customer Access:** Authenticated customers can securely `list` only their own associated data. This is facilitated by the `customerId` field within `LeaseAgreement`, `Repair`, `SalesTransaction`, and the denormalized `customerId` in `LeasePayment` documents. Customers can query these collections using `db.collection('collection_name').where('customerId', '==', firebase.auth().currentUser.uid)`, and security rules will enforce that only documents matching `request.auth.uid` are returned, preventing data leakage or unauthorized access.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
All documents within a given collection share a consistent security posture. For example:
*   `/laptop_products` and `/laptop_instances` are exclusively managed by Royaltech employees.
*   `/customers` allows employees to manage all records, while individual customers can manage their own specific record (identified by their Firebase UID).
*   Customer-specific financial or service records are structured to allow both employee oversight and customer self-service access based on `customerId`.

**Access Modeling & DBAC:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** For `Customer` entities, the `id` of the `Customer` document is assumed to match the Firebase `uid` of the authenticated customer. This enables simple path-based ownership for self-management (`/customers/{request.auth.uid}`).
*   **Hierarchical Paths for Nested Data:** `LeasePayment` is structured as a subcollection of `LeaseAgreement` (`/lease_agreements/{leaseAgreementId}/payments/{leasePaymentId}`), which is a natural fit for 1:N relationships and maintains clear data lineage.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** Roles for Royaltech employees (staff and admin) are managed through dedicated collections (`/roles_admin/{uid}`, `/roles_staff/{uid}`). The mere existence of a document at these paths for a given `uid` grants the associated role, adhering to the "Existence over Content" principle.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Entities like `LeaseAgreement`, `LaptopInstance`, `Repair`, and `SalesTransaction` utilize a dedicated `status` field to clearly define their current state, making application logic and security rules easier to write and understand.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Standardized field names (e.g., `customerId`, `laptopInstanceId`) and descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{customerId}`, `{laptopProductId}`) are used across all paths, improving predictability and maintainability.

This comprehensive approach ensures a secure, scalable, and debuggable Firestore backend for Royaltech Limited, minimizing complexity in security rules and maximizing data integrity.
